title: hieracium carpathicum subsp. glaucopallidulum
intro: Hieracium carpathicum subsp. glaucopallidulum, also known as the Carpathian hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium carpathicum plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hieracium carpathicum subsp. glaucopallidulum include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to support digestive health and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
